sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.96 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Let$M$be a free metabelian group of finite rank$r 2$, and let$P$be the set of its primitive elements. (An element of a relatively free group is said to be primitive if it can be included in a basis of the group.) [r]{a)} Is$P$a first-order definable set? [r]{b)} Is the set of bases of the group$M$a first-order definable set?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
